verses.text: The kingdom is like a householder hiring labourers early, agreeing for a penny. The call to work. God calls workers into His vineyard at His pleasure.
The kingdom is like a householder hiring labourers early, agreeing for a penny. The call to work. God calls workers into His vineyard at His pleasure.
The kingdom is like a householder hiring labourers early, agreeing for a penny. The call to work. God calls workers into His vineyard at His pleasure.
The kingdom is like a householder hiring labourers early, agreeing for a penny. The call to work. God calls workers into His vineyard at His pleasure.
He hires more at the third, sixth, ninth, and eleventh hours, sending them in. Late callings. Even those who come late are given a place.
He hires more at the third, sixth, ninth, and eleventh hours, sending them in. Late callings. Even those who come late are given a place.
He hires more at the third, sixth, ninth, and eleventh hours, sending them in. Late callings. Even those who come late are given a place.
At evening each receives a penny; the first murmur, expecting more. Surprised by grace. They resent that latecomers fare as well as they.
At evening each receives a penny; the first murmur, expecting more. Surprised by grace. They resent that latecomers fare as well as they.
At evening each receives a penny; the first murmur, expecting more. Surprised by grace. They resent that latecomers fare as well as they.
They complain that the last, who wrought one hour, are made equal to them. Envy. They begrudge the master's generosity to others.
They complain that the last, who wrought one hour, are made equal to them. Envy. They begrudge the master's generosity to others.
'Friend, I do thee no wrong'; is thine eye evil because I am good? 'the last shall be first.' Sovereign goodness. God may be gracious as He pleases, owing no one.
'Friend, I do thee no wrong'; is thine eye evil because I am good? 'the last shall be first.' Sovereign goodness. God may be gracious as He pleases, owing no one.

Going up to Jerusalem, He foretells His betrayal, mocking, crucifixion, and rising. Plain warning. He walks knowingly toward the cross.

Zebedee's sons' mother asks the chief seats; ye shall drink My cup, but the places are the Father's to give. Misplaced ambition. The way up is through the cup of suffering.

The ten are vexed; greatness is in serving; 'the Son of man came... to give his life a ransom for many.' The great pattern. Christ's whole life and death was self-giving service.

Two blind men by Jericho cry, 'Son of David, have mercy'; the crowd would silence them, but they cry the more. Persistent faith. They will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
Two blind men by Jericho cry, 'Son of David, have mercy'; the crowd would silence them, but they cry the more. Persistent faith. They will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
Two blind men by Jericho cry, 'Son of David, have mercy'; the crowd would silence them, but they cry the more. Persistent faith. They will not be hushed from seeking Christ.
He asks what they will; 'that our eyes may be opened'; He touches them, and they see and follow. Sight and discipleship. The healed at once become followers.
